content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 23, 1999


Christopher Armenta
Social Outreach of Latino Voters
10736 Jefferson Boulevard
Culver City, California 90230

Re: Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. I-99-057

Dear Mr. Armenta:

     This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please bear in mind that nothing in this letter should be
construed as evaluation of any conduct which may already have taken place. Further, this letter
is based on the facts as they have been presented to us. The Commission does not act as the
Finder of Fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) ] FPPC Ops. 71.) As noted below, we
do not have sufficient facts upon which to render formal advice. Instead and in accordance with
Regulation 18329 (copy enclosed), we are providing informal assistance to you so that you may
have some guidance in the analysis of your question.²

QUESTION

     May the Social Outreach of Latino Voters ("SOLV") send out paid political slate mailers
that promote political candidates and encourage recipients to participate in telephone prize
giveaways?

CONCLUSION

     Yes, the SOLV may send the slate mailers subject to all applicable disclosure and
reporting requirements established under the Act.

_______________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not confer upon the requestor the immunity provided by Section 83114.

File No. I-99-057
Page 2

FACTS

SOLV is planning to send out paid political slate mailers promoting political candidates.
In addition to promoting candidates, random prizes (cash or merchandise) will be given away at
no obligation to the recipients of the mailer. The recipients will be selected from a pre-
established mailing list. Each piece of mail will contain a local telephone number that the
recipient may call to determine if he or she is a random winner. During the telephone call, in
addition to hearing the names of winners, the caller will hear a brief political message. No one
may win without placing the telephone call.

ANALYSIS

Nothing in the Act prohibits the sending of slate mailers. However, we do commend to
your review the disclosure and filing requirements for slate mail organizations. (Sections
82048.3, 82048.4, and 84305.5) Copies of these statutes are enclosed for your convenience.

We cannot opine on the propriety of the "prize" procedure you have described in your
letter as the Act does not regulate this kind of activity. We do suggest that you consult with a
private lawyer or a lawyer at your local district attorney's office to determine if the prize
procedure will violate other state or federal laws.

While the Act does not prohibit slate mailers, you should be aware that the costs of the
mailer and, in this instance, the value of the prizes awarded may constitute reportable
contributions to the candidates promoted by the mailers if the mailers were sent in cooperation
with the candidates and/or at their behest. (Section 82015; Regulations 18215 and 18225.7.)
Alternatively, the costs of the mailer and the value of the prizes may be reportable independent
expenditures made on behalf of the candidates. (Section 82025; Regulation 18225.)

You have not provided us with sufficient information to determine if the costs of the
mailers and the value of the prizes would be contributions or independent expenditures or
neither. If you would like our assistance in making these determinations, please contact us again
and provide to us the names of the candidates, the nature of the candidates' involvement in the
mailer and prize procedure and the source, nature and value of the prizes.

File No. I-99-057
Page 3

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Lisa L. Ditora
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

CONCLUSION: Yes, the SOLV may send the slate mailers subject to all applicable disclosure and
